source.title: Structural Damage - Crane Lifting Incident, NABORS MODS 400

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Walker Ridge Block 29

event.top_event: Loss of Load - Casing Joint Drop
actions_taken: All personnel evacuated to designated Safe Zones
Operation Assurance Briefing (OAB) and checklist implemented before each task
JSA's revised to address hazards when using different size lift nubbins
Lift nubbins color-coded, sized, and assigned proper storage locations
Flowchart created for Stop Work procedures and task supervisor approval for restart
event.summary: On December 10, 2019, a 4,205 pound joint of 13-3/4" casing fell approximately 28 feet to the rig floor after being hoisted using an incorrectly sized 13-5/8" lift nubbin. The wrong nubbin was installed on joint number 5 despite a Floorhand calling an All Stop and a TSI Inspector visually inspecting the connection. The Inspector failed to verify the stenciled size marking on the nubbin. No injuries occurred as all personnel were in designated Safe Zones.
recommendations: Implement Operation Assurance Briefing (OAB) before each task
Revise JSA's to explicitly identify correct lift nubbin sizes for each casing size
Color-code and label lift nubbins with size designations
Establish clear flowchart for Stop Work procedures and supervisor approval for work restart
Ensure inspectors physically examine and verify equipment markings, not just visual observation

hazards.name: Multiple Casing Sizes in Single Operation

threats.name: Insufficient JSA Guidance on Nubbin Selection

consequences.name: Casing Joint Drop to Rig Floor
Impact Damage to Catwalk Machine, Casing Tongs, Iron Roughneck

controls.name: Pre-Job Safety Meeting and Plan of Action
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure crew awareness and planning
controls.lod_basis: Initial planning and communication before work begins

supporting_text: the Chevron Drill Site Representative (DSR) led the "B" drill crew through a pre-job safety meeting on the rig floor
A Plan of Action (POA) was generated by Chevron with all necessary personnel, tools, equipment, and Job Safety Analysis (JSA's)

controls.name: Designated Safe Zones for Personnel
controls.barrier_role: Engineering/Administrative control to protect personnel from dropped load consequences
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense to prevent injuries from load drop

supporting_text: Chevron also designated Safe Zones for personnel to stay in while lifting the casing
All personnel were in the designated Safe Zones and there were no injuries as a result of this incident

controls.name: Stop Work Authority - Floorhand Intervention
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to halt unsafe operations when hazards detected
controls.lod_basis: Mid-process intervention by crew member to catch errors

supporting_text: A Nabors Floor Hand suspected that the incorrect lift nubbin was installed on joint number 3 and called an "All Stop."
The TSI Inspector inspected the lift nubbin and the casing, and he confirmed (incorrectly) that the correct lift nubbin was on the casing and work resumed

controls.name: TSI Inspector Visual Inspection of Lift Nubbin
controls.barrier_role: Administrative/Human control to verify correct equipment before lifting
controls.lod_basis: Third-party inspection to catch equipment mismatches

supporting_text: the TSI Inspector visually examined the lift nubbin connection and gave approval for operations to continue
the TSI Inspector did not verify the size of the nubbin by reviewing the size stamped onto the nubbin
the TSI Inspector briefly walked up to the nubbin and looked at it, but never physically touched it or examined it in detail
If the TSI Inspector had read the stenciled information on the lift nubbin, he would have seen that it was intended for 13-5/8" casing only

controls.name: Lift Nubbin Size Stenciling
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to identify correct equipment through labeling
controls.lod_basis: Equipment design feature to prevent selection errors

supporting_text: the BSEE inspection team noted that the lift nubbins had their sizes stenciled on the top

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for Casing Operations
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to document hazards and controls for the task

supporting_text: In reviewing both Nabors and Weatherford's JSA's, there is no mention of using different size lift nubbins on this casing run

controls.name: Operation Assurance Briefing (OAB) and Checklist
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to verify equipment readiness and task procedures before work
controls.lod_basis: Enhanced pre-work planning and verification process

supporting_text: Since the incident, Chevron, Nabors and third party companies have started using the Operation Assurance Briefing (OAB) and checklist before the start of each task
The OAB is led by a Chevron DSR and attended by key Nabors personnel (Toolpusher and Driller if possible) and the main representative of the service company involved

controls.name: Lift Nubbin Color Coding and Storage Management
controls.barrier_role: Engineering/Administrative control to prevent selection of wrong nubbin
controls.lod_basis: Equipment organization and visual identification system

supporting_text: the JSA will identify the lift nubbins by color coding, size, and proper location within a safe storage area

controls.name: Stop Work Flowchart and Task Supervisor Approval
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure proper authorization before resuming work after All Stop
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to prevent unauthorized work restart

supporting_text: Chevron and Nabors have created a flowchart to indicate a flow path once "Stop Work" is called by any crew member, with the task supervisor approving the return to work before proceeding forward

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
